title: Pull recent evidence on the durability of remission in CAR-T therapy for r/r large B-cell lymphoma
subtitle: The realistic five-year picture
description: An agent pulls the long-term follow-up publications for axi-cel, tisa-cel, and liso-cel in r/r LBCL, extracts the durability metrics, and synthesizes a realistic five-year remission picture.

events.content: Pull recent evidence on the durability of remission in CAR-T therapy for relapsed/refractory large B-cell lymphoma. What's the realistic five-year picture?
Three approved products — axi-cel (ZUMA-1), tisa-cel (JULIET), liso-cel (TRANSCEND). I need the longest-follow-up publications for each, plus any real-world registry data.

events.args: paperclip search "CAR-T axicabtagene tisagenlecleucel lisocabtagene long-term follow-up large B-cell lymphoma" -n 18

events.content:
Found 16 documents  [s_cart_lbcl]

  Sources: pmc(10), trials/us(4), fda(2)

  1. NEJM 2017 — ZUMA-1 primary publication (Neelapu)
  2. Lancet Oncol 2023 — ZUMA-1 5-year follow-up
  3. NEJM 2019 — JULIET primary (Schuster)
  4. J Clin Oncol 2023 — JULIET 5-year update
  5. Lancet 2020 — TRANSCEND primary (Abramson)
  6. Blood 2024 — TRANSCEND long-term follow-up
  7. NEJM 2022 — ZUMA-7 (2L axi-cel vs SOC)
  8. NEJM 2022 — TRANSFORM (2L liso-cel vs SOC)
  9. NEJM 2023 — BELINDA (2L tisa-cel vs SOC)
 10. JCO 2024 — CIBMTR real-world axi-cel registry analysis
 11. Blood 2024 — EBMT real-world tisa/axi pooled long-term
 12. FDA Multi-Disc Review — axi-cel sBLA (long-term safety)
  ... [4 more]

events.args:
paperclip map --from s_cart_lbcl \
  "Extract for r/r LBCL: (1) product, (2) line of therapy, (3) median follow-up, (4) best ORR / CR, (5) durable response rate (PFS or EFS at 24, 36, 60 months), (6) any conditional landmark analysis findings."

events.content:
Map complete: 12/12 documents  [m_cart_lbcl]

  ✓ ZUMA-1 5-year follow-up (Lancet Oncol 2023)
    Product: axi-cel. Line: 3L+. Median f/u: 63.1 months.
    Best ORR 83%, CR 58%. 5-year PFS 31.2%. Of patients in
    CR at month 12, 76% remained progression-free at 5 years.
    Plateau apparent from ~month 24.

  ✓ JULIET 5-year update (J Clin Oncol 2023)
    Product: tisa-cel. Line: 3L+. Median f/u: 60.8 months.
    Best ORR 53%, CR 39%. 5-year PFS 19.5%. Landmark CR at 12mo:
    65% progression-free at 5 years.

  ✓ TRANSCEND long-term (Blood 2024)
    Product: liso-cel. Line: 3L+. Median f/u: 41.0 months.
    Best ORR 73%, CR 53%. 36-month PFS 36.6%. Conditional CR
    landmark at 12mo: 70% remain in remission at 36mo.

  ✓ ZUMA-7 (NEJM 2022)
    Product: axi-cel. Line: 2L. Primary EFS at median 24.9mo
    follow-up: HR 0.40 vs SOC, 24-month EFS 41% vs 16%.

  ✓ TRANSFORM (NEJM 2022)
    Product: liso-cel. Line: 2L. Primary EFS HR 0.35 vs SOC.
    18-month EFS 52.6% vs 20.8%.

  ✓ BELINDA (NEJM 2023)
    Product: tisa-cel. Line: 2L. Did NOT meet primary EFS
    endpoint vs SOC. HR 1.07. Differs from ZUMA-7/TRANSFORM
    — manufacturing timeline differences likely contributory.

  ✓ CIBMTR real-world axi-cel registry (JCO 2024)
    N=1,500+ commercial-use patients. 24-month PFS 35%,
    similar to trial. Cytopenias and infection were dominant
    long-term safety concerns; rare secondary T-cell malignancy
    signal under active investigation.

  ✓ EBMT real-world tisa/axi pooled (Blood 2024)
    24-month PFS 31% (axi) vs 24% (tisa). CRS grade 3+ rate
    lower in real-world than trial (~9% vs ~13%).

The picture is clear: axi-cel and liso-cel both show durable plateau ~30-40% PFS at long follow-up; tisa-cel slightly lower in 3L+ and did not beat SOC in 2L. Real-world data largely confirms trial estimates. Late safety concerns now include the secondary malignancy signal.

## CAR-T durability in r/r large B-cell lymphoma — the five-year picture

The long-term follow-up data now spans 4–5 years for all three approved products. The durable-remission rate has stabilized in a **roughly 30–40% range** at 5 years for axi-cel and liso-cel, with tisa-cel modestly lower in 3L+ and failing to beat SOC in 2L. Real-world registry data confirms the trial estimates, with a few new safety signals emerging in long-term follow-up.

### Durability by product

| Product | Trial | Median F/U | 5-yr PFS (3L+) | Landmark — CR @ 12mo → durable |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| axi-cel | ZUMA-1 | 63 mo | **31%** | 76% remain progression-free at 5 years |
| tisa-cel | JULIET | 61 mo | **20%** | 65% remain progression-free at 5 years |
| liso-cel | TRANSCEND | 41 mo | **37% (3-yr)** | 70% remain in remission at 36 months |

All three show a **PFS plateau emerging at ~24 months** — patients who remain progression-free at the two-year landmark have a substantially higher conditional probability of long-term remission than the headline PFS rate suggests.

### Second-line vs third-line

- **ZUMA-7 (axi-cel, 2L)**: HR 0.40 vs SOC, 24-month EFS 41% vs 16%. Positive.
- **TRANSFORM (liso-cel, 2L)**: HR 0.35 vs SOC, 18-month EFS 53% vs 21%. Positive.
- **BELINDA (tisa-cel, 2L)**: did **not** meet primary endpoint vs SOC. Manufacturing-timeline differences (median time-to-infusion was meaningfully longer for tisa-cel) are the leading explanation.

### Real-world confirmation

- CIBMTR axi-cel (n > 1,500 commercial-use): 24-month PFS **35%**, closely aligned with trial estimate.
- EBMT pooled axi-cel + tisa-cel: 24-month PFS 31% (axi) vs 24% (tisa), with CRS grade 3+ lower in real-world (~9%) than in trial (~13%) — likely due to operator learning curve and prophylactic tocilizumab use.

### Late safety signals worth flagging

1. **Prolonged cytopenias and infection** are the dominant long-term safety burden.
2. **Secondary T-cell malignancy signal** is under active FDA investigation; the absolute risk remains low but the temporal relationship to the CAR construct has triggered class-wide label discussion.
3. **Hypogammaglobulinemia** persisting beyond 12 months in many patients — IVIG support is an ongoing care burden.

### What to tell a patient

A realistic counseling framing for an r/r LBCL patient considering CAR-T:

- *Roughly one in three patients will have a durable remission at five years.*
- *If you remain in CR at one year, the conditional probability of remaining in remission at five years is approximately three in four.*
- *Early toxicity (CRS, neurotoxicity) is well-managed in experienced centers; the harder long-term burden is cytopenias, infections, and immunoglobulin replacement.*
- *A secondary malignancy signal is under active investigation; the absolute risk appears low.*

The field has settled into a stable evidence base — the next inflection will be earlier-line use, fixed-vs-conditioned manufacturing, and the off-the-shelf allogeneic alternatives now in late-stage development.

*Documents read: 12. Wall clock: 214 seconds.*
